title: Ravens React to Blockbuster Trades: Garrett, Brown Moves Analyzed
body:
The NFL landscape is constantly evolving, with offseason moves and trades reshaping the prospects of teams across the league. For the Baltimore Ravens, the recent blockbuster trades involving Myles Garrett and A.J. Brown have significant implications, particularly in terms of their own chances of returning to the playoffs in the 2026 season. With Garrett, a dominant defensive force, now out of the AFC North, the Ravens may have dodged a bullet, as they will no longer face him twice a season. Meanwhile, pundits are speculating about the timeline for quarterback Lamar Jackson's contract extension, a crucial factor in the team's long-term success.

From a Ravens perspective, the Garrett trade is a welcome development, as it removes a major obstacle from their path. As one of the most feared pass-rushers in the league, Garrett had the potential to wreak havoc on the Ravens' offense, particularly in divisional matchups. His departure from the AFC North means that the Ravens will no longer have to contend with his game-wrecking abilities on a regular basis, which could significantly improve their prospects in the division. Furthermore, with the Ravens tabbed as the most likely non-playoff team from 2025 to return to the postseason, the removal of Garrett from the equation can only be seen as a positive.

The Ravens' chances of returning to the playoffs are also bolstered by the presence of several key players who have been identified as among the most important in the league for the 2026 season. With four Ravens featuring in the top 100, the team has a solid foundation to build upon. This core of talented players, combined with the potential for Jackson to lead the team to victory, makes the Ravens a dark horse candidate to make a deep run in the playoffs. However, the key to unlocking this potential lies in securing Jackson's long-term commitment to the team, which is why the speculation surrounding his contract extension is so significant.

The contract negotiations between Jackson and the Ravens are likely to be a major storyline in the coming weeks and months. As one of the most dynamic and talented quarterbacks in the league, Jackson is a vital component of the Ravens' offense, and his absence would be deeply felt. The Ravens will be keen to secure his signature on a long-term deal, not only to ensure continuity and stability but also to provide a sense of security and confidence for the team. With the NFL being a quarterback-driven league, the Ravens' chances of success are inextricably linked to Jackson's performance, making the outcome of these negotiations crucial to their prospects.

As the Ravens look to the 2026 season, they will be buoyed by the knowledge that they have a talented core of players and a favorable schedule. The absence of Garrett from the division will undoubtedly make their lives easier, and the team will be looking to capitalize on this advantage to make a push for the playoffs. With Jackson at the helm and a solid supporting cast, the Ravens have the potential to be a major force in the league, and their fans will be eager to see how the season unfolds.

Looking ahead to the 2026 season, the Ravens have a lot to be optimistic about. With a talented roster, a favorable schedule, and the potential for Jackson to lead the team to victory, the team has a genuine chance of making a deep run in the playoffs. While there are still many uncertainties and challenges to overcome, the Ravens are well-placed to make a significant impact in the league. As the team prepares to take the field, their fans will be watching with bated breath, eager to see how the season unfolds and whether the Ravens can live up to their potential.
